verdict_reasoning: TMED10/p24δ1 is a type I single-pass TM protein of the p24 family, predominantly resident in the early secretory pathway (ER-Golgi cisternae, ERGIC) where it functions as a cargo receptor cycling between ER and Golgi. NCBI explicitly notes plasma membrane localization in addition to Golgi, consistent with a documented minority PM pool from secretory cycling — the cytosolic tail faces the cytoplasm and the GOLD-domain ectodomain faces the lumen, which becomes extracellular upon reaching the PM. This qualifies as dual_localization: dominant endomembrane home with a documented PM fraction reached via its own TM anchor.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Size and accessibility of the PM pool relative to dominant Golgi/ERGIC residence is unclear; surface display may be transient and minor.
